manifest.title: U.S.S. Breckenridge: Ashes of Peace - Open World

sourceDocuments.title: Directive Ashes of Peace Campaign v0.2

ship.name: U.S.S. Breckenridge
ship.openingCondition: Returned to service after a four-month repair and modernization period at Utopia Planitia; certified for service with several upgraded systems still requiring integrated validation under sustained deployment conditions.

commandStructure.playerRole: Principal mission commander and coordinator of shipboard operations

serviceHistory.commissioning: Entered service approximately three years before the campaign.
serviceHistory.wartimeService: Survey, patrol, diplomatic-support, convoy escort, rapid-response, and reconnaissance duty before a major yard period.

yardPeriod.location: Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards, Mars orbit

openProductionDecisions: Exact late-war action that forced the yard period

travelContinuity.openingTransitMode: The Breckenridge has been underway from the Mars/Utopia Planitia refit context for twenty-five days before the player joins, using a conservative warp flight plan through Federation territory.
travelContinuity.openingImpulseContext: At the opening, the ship drops to impulse at a charted transfer waypoint only to receive the player's long-range Starfleet personnel shuttle.
travelContinuity.openingShuttleApproach: The player's shuttle approaches from astern and aligns with shuttlebay two in the aft section between the swept nacelle pylons. Do not depict the Intrepid-class Breckenridge as having a saucer-underside shuttlebay for this arrival.
travelContinuity.baselineRemainingTravel: The player joins with a baseline ten-day prelude remaining before the Asterion Reach.
travelContinuity.baselineRemainingDistance: The remaining prelude represents roughly 5.5 to 6 light-years of travel in stable Directive continuity.
travelContinuity.speedPolicy: Observe the standing Warp 5 restriction where practical, use lower speeds in sensitive or heavily traveled corridors, and reserve sustained high warp for operational need.

postRefitWork: Structural repair and recalibration of nacelle articulation
Replacement and recalibration of damaged bio-neural gel packs
Revised tactical software compatible with postwar countermeasure libraries
Warp-field wake monitoring and nacelle-control diagnostics

knownTechnicalDebt.label: Integrated validation pending
knownTechnicalDebt.playerSafeSummary: The Breckenridge is certified for service, but its upgraded systems have not yet been tested together under sustained deployment conditions. High-stress operations may reveal interactions that routine checks missed.

knownTechnicalDebt.label: Command-network certificate mismatch
knownTechnicalDebt.playerSafeSummary: Command-network certificate compatibility issue exposed during recent shakedown checks between revised authentication systems and shipboard control paths. Secure command handoffs may take extra verification until Engineering remediates it.

knownTechnicalDebt.label: Combined refit load untested
knownTechnicalDebt.playerSafeSummary: Several refit discrepancies are within certified limits individually, but have not been validated together under maximum anticipated mission load. Command may need conservative operating choices when multiple upgraded systems are stressed at once.

senior.packageRole: Strategic authority and final legal command
senior.publicProfile: Captain Mara Whitaker is Human, Commanding Officer.

senior.name: Kieran Vale
senior.packageRole: Gifted test pilot; ambition, mentorship, and controlled risk
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Kieran Vale is Human, Flight Control Officer.

senior.name: Priya Nayar
senior.packageRole: Coordination, informal influence, and ethical boundaries
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Priya Nayar is Human, Operations Officer.

senior.name: Hadrik Bronn

senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Commander Hadrik Bronn is the Breckenridge's veteran Tellarite tactical and security chief, original senior officer, and temporary acting XO during the post-refit transit.
senior.appearanceSummary: Broad, compact Tellarite officer with russet skin, a strong snout, heavy brows, cropped bristling hair, and a neatly squared beard.
publicIdentityFacts: Original Breckenridge senior officer and acting XO during the post-refit transit.

senior.name: Rowan Saye
senior.packageRole: Evidentiary rigor, dissent, and institutional skepticism
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Commander Rowan Saye is Human, Chief Science Officer.

senior.name: Miriam Sato
senior.packageRole: Medical reality, bioethics, casualty burden, and honest cost
senior.publicProfile: Commander Miriam Sato is Human, Chief Medical Officer.

senior.name: Imani Cross
senior.packageRole: Technical integrity, autonomy, identity, and long-term consequence
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Commander Imani Cross is Human, Chief Engineer.

cohorts.label: Returning Breckenridge Personnel
cohorts.summary: A small body of returning veterans with institutional memory of the ship's first three years.

cohorts.label: Patrol and Escort Transfer Cohort
cohorts.summary: A large transfer cohort accustomed to wartime readiness, austerity, and direct chains of command.

cohorts.label: Science and Support Transfer Cohort
cohorts.summary: A large transfer cohort accustomed to extended research, civilian coordination, and procedural consultation.

cohorts.label: Individual Specialists
cohorts.summary: Specialists selected for Intrepid-class operations, post-refit systems, or the Asterion assignment.

relationshipModel.startingFrame: The crew has spent twenty-five days underway together; the player is new to the command culture.

campaignContext.missionProfile: Postwar reconstruction, relief coordination, diplomatic support, patrol, survey, and investigation in the Asterion Reach.
campaignContext.playerRoleRule: Locked role: incoming permanent Commander and Executive Officer, serving as principal mission commander under Captain Mara Whitaker.

lockedRole.commandAuthority: Principal mission commander and coordinator of shipboard operations.
lockedRole.captainAuthorityBoundary: Captain Whitaker retains final legal command and may approve, refuse, or counteroffer major mission deviations.

ageBands.label: Young for the role
ageBands.summary: A comparatively new Commander whose authority is credible but still invites scrutiny.

ageBands.label: Mid-career
ageBands.summary: A proven officer with enough experience to be trusted and enough room to keep changing.

ageBands.label: Experienced
ageBands.summary: A seasoned Commander with a substantial record before the Breckenridge assignment.

ageBands.label: Late-career
ageBands.summary: An older officer whose long service may invite respect, assumptions, or questions about this posting.

allowedSpecies.label: Human
allowedSpecies.summary: A common Starfleet background with broad cultural flexibility.

allowedSpecies.label: Vulcan
allowedSpecies.summary: A Federation officer whose logic, discipline, and emotional reserve may shape command perception.

allowedSpecies.label: Bajoran
allowedSpecies.summary: A post-occupation Federation-aligned officer whose recent history may resonate with reconstruction politics.

allowedSpecies.label: Betazoid
allowedSpecies.summary: A Federation officer whose empathy and social perception can matter without replacing evidence.

allowedSpecies.label: Trill
allowedSpecies.summary: A Starfleet officer from a culture with strong traditions of memory, identity, and service.

allowedSpecies.label: Andorian
allowedSpecies.summary: A Federation officer whose directness, duty, and tactical instincts may affect first impressions.

allowedSpecies.label: Tellarite
allowedSpecies.summary: A Federation officer from a combative rhetorical culture, useful for disciplined disagreement.

allowedSpecies.label: Bolian
allowedSpecies.summary: A Federation officer from a service culture often associated with sociability and institutional trust.

allowedSpecies.label: Custom Federation species
allowedSpecies.summary: A player-defined Federation or Starfleet-compatible species that must remain plausible for the era and assignment.

careerBackgrounds.label: Command and administration
careerBackgrounds.summary: Staff coordination, duty structure, officer management, reports, and command process.

careerBackgrounds.label: Operations and logistics
careerBackgrounds.summary: Resource allocation, scheduling, supply, routing, and making complicated ships function as a whole.

careerBackgrounds.label: Tactical and security
careerBackgrounds.summary: Threat assessment, security posture, weapons readiness, boarding risk, and disciplined force.

careerBackgrounds.label: Flight and navigation
careerBackgrounds.summary: Helm operations, route planning, shiphandling, traffic control, and spatial judgment.

careerBackgrounds.label: Science and exploration
careerBackgrounds.summary: Survey work, anomaly assessment, evidence discipline, and field research coordination.

careerBackgrounds.label: Engineering and systems
careerBackgrounds.summary: Technical operations, damage control, system constraints, and reliability under pressure.

careerBackgrounds.label: Medical and humanitarian service
careerBackgrounds.summary: Relief work, triage realities, bioethics, evacuation, and civilian duty of care.

careerBackgrounds.label: Diplomacy and first contact
careerBackgrounds.summary: Negotiation, cultural contact, local authority, protocol, and coalition-building.

careerBackgrounds.label: Intelligence and strategic analysis
careerBackgrounds.summary: Pattern analysis, compartmentalized information, counter-deception, and institutional caution.

formativeExperiences.label: Dominion War fleet service
formativeExperiences.summary: Direct wartime Starfleet experience that shaped how the officer thinks about risk, duty, and peace.

formativeExperiences.label: Disaster relief and evacuation
formativeExperiences.summary: Major civilian-response work where lives depended on logistics, calm, and hard prioritization.

formativeExperiences.label: Frontier or border service
formativeExperiences.summary: Work in thinly supported regions where formal authority and practical authority often diverged.

formativeExperiences.label: Convoy and logistics duty
formativeExperiences.summary: Protection, routing, supply, and the unglamorous work that keeps people alive.

formativeExperiences.label: Deep-space exploration
formativeExperiences.summary: Extended operations far from help, with unknown conditions and long chains of consequence.

formativeExperiences.label: First-contact or diplomatic duty
formativeExperiences.summary: Assignments where careful speech, cultural judgment, and credibility mattered as much as orders.

formativeExperiences.label: Scientific expedition
formativeExperiences.summary: Evidence-led field work where the officer learned patience, humility, and disciplined uncertainty.

formativeExperiences.label: Starbase or planetary assignment
formativeExperiences.summary: Institutional service close to civilian life, politics, maintenance, and long-running local obligations.

formativeExperiences.label: Training or staff service
formativeExperiences.summary: Instruction, readiness planning, personnel evaluation, and learning how institutions reproduce culture.

formativeExperiences.label: Routine professional service
formativeExperiences.summary: A credible record without a single defining crisis, leaving the character's deeper history open.

assignmentReasons.label: Requested by the captain
assignmentReasons.summary: Whitaker wanted this officer for the permanent XO billet, though the exact reason may be simple or layered.

assignmentReasons.label: Relevant specialist experience
assignmentReasons.summary: Starfleet selected the officer because their past service fits the Asterion Reach assignment.

assignmentReasons.label: Promoted into the role
assignmentReasons.summary: The assignment is both a recognition of readiness and an immediate test of command judgment.

assignmentReasons.label: Experienced outsider transfer
assignmentReasons.summary: The officer brings fresh perspective to a crew that has only recently begun to settle.

assignmentReasons.label: Requested a fresh start
assignmentReasons.summary: The officer asked for a posting where they could do meaningful work outside a previous pattern.

assignmentReasons.label: Reassigned after disagreement
assignmentReasons.summary: A prior professional conflict made the transfer useful, but the assignment remains honorable.

assignmentReasons.label: Part of a newly assembled crew
assignmentReasons.summary: Starfleet assigned the officer because the Breckenridge needed a stable command spine after reconstitution.

assignmentReasons.label: Let the creator decide
assignmentReasons.summary: The dossier generator may choose a restrained, plausible reason from the campaign context.

assignmentReasons.label: Custom
assignmentReasons.summary: The player supplies a concise reason that fits Starfleet service and the campaign's locked XO role.

options.label: Analytical
options.summary: Breaks problems into evidence, patterns, and constraints.

options.label: Perceptive
options.summary: Notices behavior, context, and subtle shifts in a scene.

options.label: Curious
options.summary: Asks useful questions and resists premature certainty.

options.label: Intuitive
options.summary: Forms practical reads from incomplete but meaningful signals.

options.label: Diplomatic
options.summary: Finds language that preserves room for cooperation.

options.label: Empathic
options.summary: Pays attention to dignity, pain, fear, and hope.

options.label: Candid
options.summary: Builds credibility through direct, respectful honesty.

options.label: Inspiring
options.summary: Helps others find confidence, purpose, and shared resolve.

options.label: Decisive
options.summary: Acts when delay would become its own risk.

options.label: Disciplined
options.summary: Keeps standards and process intact under pressure.

options.label: Resourceful
options.summary: Makes good use of limited tools, people, time, and information.

options.label: Patient
options.summary: Lets evidence, people, or systems mature before forcing a result.

options.label: Guarded
options.summary: Protects private reasoning or feelings longer than others might prefer.

options.label: Stubborn
options.summary: Can overcommit to a course after deciding it is right.

options.label: Impatient
options.summary: Struggles when process, politics, or fear slow necessary action.

options.label: Controlling
options.summary: May have difficulty delegating when the stakes feel personal.

options.label: Proud
options.summary: Can mistake personal credibility for mission necessity.

options.label: Distrustful
options.summary: Requires evidence and consistency before granting confidence.

options.label: Overprotective
options.summary: May shield others from risk even when trust requires sharing it.

options.label: Rigid
options.summary: Leans heavily on principles, procedure, or doctrine when ambiguity rises.

must: Use the active campaign era, ship, role, and mission profile.
Treat player-entered facts as authoritative unless they contradict package guardrails.
Keep rank and service progression plausible for a Starfleet Commander assigned as XO.
Use no more than two named prior postings by default.
Explain why the Breckenridge assignment is credible.
Leave family, private beliefs, romantic history, and old relationships open unless the player supplied them.
Treat Inspiration and Resolve as earned in play, not selected during creation.
mustNot: Invent secret ancestry, hidden powers, criminal history, severe trauma, or canonical celebrity ties without explicit player request.
Establish friendships, rivalries, or romances with current Breckenridge crew before play begins.
Reveal campaign secrets or information unavailable at stardate 53049.2.
Make the player character universally admired or exceptional in every field.
Let the player character replace senior specialists in advanced engineering, medicine, science, piloting, tactical execution, or intelligence work.
Assume a different ship, captain, campaign, or player role.

continuityGuardrails.label: XO authority
continuityGuardrails.summary: The character is the Breckenridge's incoming permanent XO, not the captain or a civilian specialist.

continuityGuardrails.label: Captain retains command
continuityGuardrails.summary: Whitaker can delegate substantial mission authority but retains final legal command.

continuityGuardrails.label: New to the crew
continuityGuardrails.summary: The player character does not begin with deep relationships aboard the Breckenridge unless explicitly approved later.

continuityGuardrails.label: Postwar plausibility
continuityGuardrails.summary: Backstory should fit Starfleet service around stardate 53049.2 and the immediate post-Dominion-War period.

continuityGuardrails.label: Campaign secret safety
continuityGuardrails.summary: The character cannot begin knowing Pale Lantern, Farwatch truths, hidden faction clocks, or undiscovered mission evidence.

continuityGuardrails.label: Specialist boundary
continuityGuardrails.summary: The character may have broad competence, but senior staff remain necessary experts in their departments.

localFallback.biographyTemplate: {{name}} is a {{species}} Starfleet Commander assigned as Executive Officer of the U.S.S. Breckenridge on stardate 53049.2. Their background in {{careerBackground}} and formative experience with {{formativeExperience}} made them a credible choice for the Asterion Reach mission. Their command style is shaped by {{insightTrait}}, {{connectionTrait}}, and {{executionTrait}}, while {{flaw}} remains a pressure point they will need to manage in command.

campaign.title: Ashes of Peace
campaign.highConcept:
The war is over. In the Asterion Reach, that only means no one is sure who is still fighting. Relief convoys vanish from clean routes. Starfleet signals arrive from ships that never sent them. Civil authorities who held whole worlds together during the Dominion crisis now face a Federation that needs their trust and may have already lost it.

Into that fracture comes the U.S.S. Breckenridge: newly refit, politically exposed, and crewed by officers still learning how to become a command team. As the incoming executive officer, you inherit the first impossible question of the mission: who is using Starfleet's voice to move cargo, threaten witnesses, and turn neighbor against neighbor?

Every answer risks lighting the next fuse. Cardassian refugees, Compact patrols, damaged colonies, and Starfleet observers all have reasons to bury part of the truth. To keep the Reach from sliding back into war, you will need to read the evidence, command the ship, and decide which peace is worth defending before someone else writes the next battle in your name.
campaign.thesis: Peace is the work of deciding what must be repaired, what must be changed, and who gets to participate in that decision.

cards.title: Open-World Direction
cards.content: The Reach is a persistent region, not a chapter corridor. Preserve player choice of activity, allow postponement and refusal, and advance only causally grounded offscreen actors and fronts.
cards.text: The Reach is a persistent region, not a chapter corridor. Preserve player choice of activity, allow postponement and refusal, and advance only causally grounded offscreen actors and fronts.

cards.title: Pale Lantern Truth
cards.content: Pale Lantern is a distributed Dominion contingency architecture. It fabricates evidence, redirects traffic, manipulates authentication, models doctrine, and gives each actor a different reason to preempt. It exploits real grievances and has no single mastermind.
cards.text: Pale Lantern is a distributed Dominion contingency architecture. It fabricates evidence, redirects traffic, manipulates authentication, models doctrine, and gives each actor a different reason to preempt. It exploits real grievances and has no single mastermind.

cards.title: Calm Content
cards.content: Some repairs, personal scenes, scientific work, legal problems, and civilian concerns are ordinary. Do not reveal a Pale Lantern connection merely to increase drama.

cards.content: No actor begins with the complete campaign truth. Actor action must use recorded knowledge, goals, location, authority, and available channels.

canonEra.voyagerStatus: Voyager remains in the Delta Quadrant and does not intersect the Breckenridge campaign.

missionDirectionRules: Situation on rails, solution off rails.
Every dynamic event must have a causal parent.
Calm side assignments and shipboard scenes may remain unrelated to Pale Lantern.
Crew disagreement should be focused; not every officer gives a moral essay before action.
No character begins with the complete campaign truth.
Move information only when causally justified if the player bypasses an expected scene.
Escalation pacing should serve the situation rather than force combat or countdowns.
Factions act between player scenes based on their goals and information.
failurePolicy: No single failed check ends a mission.
Campaign-critical survival must preserve meaningful continuation after major losses or refusals.
Senior-crew death requires established lethal situation, meaningful decision or accumulated consequence, fair response opportunity, and narrative weight.
Refusing an assignment is a consequential command act, not an out-of-bounds input.
Delay advances clocks only when actors or physical processes have a reason to continue.
